parameters.content:
Figma fire event - ping slack

### How it works

This workflow monitors three Figma event types: library published, file comment, and file deleted. Each trigger runs through its own formatting code node to create an appropriate Slack message, then all formatted messages converge into a single Slack posting node. It provides an internal event log in Slack for key Figma activity.

### Setup steps

- Configure the Figma trigger credentials and webhook/event subscriptions for library publish, file comment, and file delete events.
- Configure the Slack credentials on the Post to Slack node and select the target workspace and channel.
- Review each formatting code node to confirm it matches the actual Figma payload fields and produces the Slack message format expected by the Slack node.
- Activate the workflow and test each Figma event type to verify the notification reaches Slack.

### Customization

Adjust the three formatting code nodes to change message text, include links or metadata, mention users, or add routing logic. The Slack node can be pointed to a different channel for staging, production, or event-specific logging.

## Library publish event

Top lane that listens for Figma library publish events and formats the event details into a Slack-ready message.

## File comment event

Middle lane that listens for new Figma file comments and converts the comment payload into the standard Slack message structure.

## File deletion event

Bottom lane that listens for Figma file deletion events and formats the deletion details for Slack notification.

## Shared Slack notification

Shared output node on the right that receives formatted messages from all three Figma event lanes and posts them to Slack.
